text:
PATHOLOGY REPORT - INVASIVE BREAST CARCINOMA

Patient Information: 52-year-old female
Specimen: Left breast excisional biopsy, upper outer quadrant
Date: 2026-02-28

Gross Description: Tan-white tissue measuring 4 x 3 x 2 cm with a central firm grey-white lesion.

Microscopic Diagnosis: 

PRIMARY MALIGNANCY: Invasive Ductal Carcinoma, Not Otherwise Specified (IDC-NOS)

Histological Grade: Grade 2 (Intermediate)
- Tubule formation: Mixed (30-75% tubules)
- Nuclear pleomorphism: Moderate
- Mitotic count: 8-10 per 10 HPF

Tumor Size: 1.8 cm in greatest dimension

Margins: All margins negative for malignancy (minimum 3 mm)

Associated Lesions: Ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) associated with invasive tumor, high nuclear grade

Lymphocyte Response: Moderate peritumoral lymphocytic infiltrate

Vascular Invasion: Not identified on routine sections

Additional Features: Adjacent benign breast parenchyma shows fibrosis and chronic inflammation.

Summary: 1.8 cm IDC Grade 2 with high-grade DCIS. ER/PR positive (by IHC). HER2 negative (1+ score). Ki67 proliferation index 20%.

Pathologic Stage: T1b, NX, MX (based on size)

Recommended Follow-up: Sentinel lymph node biopsy, hormonal therapy consideration, genetic counseling.

COMPREHENSIVE PATHOLOGY EXAMINATION

Case #: BR-2026-0452
Patient Age: 63 years
Specimen Type: Mastectomy with axillary dissection
Procedure Date: 2026-03-01

Clinical Indication: Invasive breast cancer with axillary lymph node involvement

MACRO FINDINGS:
Breast: 12 x 10 x 6 cm. Tumor in upper outer quadrant measuring 3.2 x 2.8 x 2.5 cm, firm, tan-grey with white streaking.

MICRO FINDINGS & DIAGNOSES:

1. PRIMARY TUMOR: Invasive Lobular Carcinoma (ILC)
   - Grade: 2 (Low to intermediate)
   - Size: 3.2 cm
   - Pattern: Single-file infiltration with desmoplastic response

2. IN SITU COMPONENT: High-grade DCIS present
   - Nuclear grade: 3 (high)
   - Necrosis: Central comedonecrosis present
   - Extent: Involving 1.5 cm of surrounding tissue

3. LYMPH NODE ASSESSMENT:
   - Total nodes examined: 14
   - Positive nodes: 3 (with metastatic carcinoma)
   - Largest metastasis: 1.2 cm
   - Extranodal extension: Present in one node

4. BIOMARKERS:
   - ER/PR: Both positive (95% and 90% cells respectively)
   - HER2: Negative (1+ score)
   - Ki67: 28% positive nuclei

5. MARGINS: Tumor close to superior margin (1 mm), clear of all others

PATHOLOGIC STAGING:
pT2 (3.2 cm), pN1 (3 positive nodes), M0
Stage IIIA

RECOMMENDATIONS: 
Systemic therapy with endocrine treatment indicated. HER2-targeted therapy not recommended.
Follow-up imaging and CA 15.3 monitoring.

DIAGNOSTIC SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient ID: AC-7834
Age: 45 years, Female  
Specimen: Core needle biopsy, right breast, medial aspect
Date Received: 2026-02-25

MACROSCOPIC:
Multiple tan-pink tissue fragments totaling 1.2 cm, consistent with breast tissue and fibrous stroma.

MICROSCOPIC EXAMINATION:

PRINCIPAL DIAGNOSIS: Invasive Ductal Carcinoma (invasive carcinoma of no special type - NST)

Grade: 3 (High Grade)
- Tubule formation: Minimal (<5% tubules)
- Nuclear pleomorphism: Marked nuclear atypia and variation
- Mitotic activity: Very high (15+ mitoses per 10 HPF)

Secondary Findings:
1. High-grade DCIS is present, extensive involvement
   - Area: Involves approximately 60% of biopsy sample
   - Pattern: Solid and comedo-type
   - Necrosis: Marked central necrosis with calcifications

2. Tumor Infiltration Pattern: 
   - Solid nests of tumor cells
   - Marked stromal response with lymphocytic infiltration
   - Perineural invasion: Identified

3. Immunohistochemical Findings:
   - ER: Positive in 65% of cells (weak to moderate intensity)
   - PR: Positive in 50% of cells (weak intensity)
   - HER2: Positive 3+ (>30% membrane staining) - HER2 amplified
   - HER2 FISH: Pending
   - Ki67: 45% positive nuclei (high proliferation)

PERITUMORAL MILIEU:
Robin's lymphocyte infiltrate with plasma cells and histiocytes.

CLINICAL CORRELATION NEEDED:
Further staging with imaging recommended. HER2 FISH confirmation needed. Neoadjuvant chemotherapy with HER2-targeted therapy may be appropriate.

STAGE: T1-2, Nx, Mx (approximately 1.2-1.5 cm tumor)
Grade: 3/3 (high grade)

SURGICAL PATHOLOGY EXAMINATION

Case: SX-2026-0681  
Patient: 58-year-old female
Specimen: Excisional biopsy with wide local margins, left breast
Date of Exam: 2026-03-02

GROSS:
Tan-white fibrofatty tissue measuring 5 x 4 x 2.5 cm. Inked margins: superior (blue), inferior (red), medial (black), lateral (green), anterior (yellow), posterior (no ink). Central lesion appears firm and grey-tan, measuring approximately 1.5 x 1.2 x 1 cm.

MICROSCOPIC EXAMINATION:

PRI▁MARY MALIGNANCY:
Invasive Carcinoma with Mixed Ductal and Lobular Features

Histological Components:
1. IDC Component (60% of tumor): Grade 2, with tubule formation and moderate nuclear atypia
2. ILC Component (40% of tumor): Infiltrative single-file pattern with desmoplasia

Combined Overall Grade: Grade 2 (Intermediate)
- Nuclear grade: 2 
- Mitotic rate: 7-8 per 10 HPF
- Tubule formation: 40%

Associated DCIS: Present, intermediate nuclear grade, extent 8 mm

MARGINS: 
- Superior (blue): Clear, 3.5 mm
- Inferior (red): Clear, 4 mm
- Medial (black): 1 mm clearance
- Lateral (green): Clear, 2.5 mm
- Anterior (yellow): 0.5 mm clearance
- Posterior: No ink, estimated 2 mm from inked edge

BIOCHEMICAL MARKERS:
- ER/PR: Both positive (ER 88%, PR 80%)
- HER2: 1+ (negative)
- Ki67: 17% proliferation index

LYMPHOCYTIC RESPONSE: Moderate

LYMPHOVASC infiltration: Not identified

DIMENSIONS: 1.5 cm invasive tumor

STAGE: pT1b, Nx, Mx

CONCLUSION: Early-stage mixed IDC/ILC with favorable hormone receptor profile. Adequate margins achieved. Sentinel lymph node biopsy recommended.

COMPLETE PATHOLOGIC ASSESSMENT - BREAST SPECIMEN

Patient: 71-year-old post-menopausal female
Case Number: LM-2026-0195
Specimen: Mastectomy with sentinel and axillary lymph node dissection
Date: 2026-02-24

CLINICAL SUMMARY:
Patient presenting with mammographically detected abnormality in right breast. Mammography showed suspicious densities and microcalcifications in upper outer quadrant.

SPECIMEN DESCRIPTION:
Breast tissue 14 x 11 x 7 cm with skin flap. Primary tumor identified in upper outer quadrant measuring 2.2 x 2 x 1.8 cm, firm, grey-tan.

HISTOPATHOLOGIC FINDINGS:

1. PRIMARY TUMOR: Invasive Ductal Carcinoma
   Grade: 1-2 (Low to low-intermediate grade)
   - Well-formed tubules: 60%
   - Minimal nuclear pleomorphism
   - Mitotic count: 3-4 per 10 HPF
   Size: 2.2 cm

2. IN SITU COMPONENT: Low to intermediate grade DCIS
   - Micropapillary and cribriform patterns
   - Extent: Involves 1 cm surrounding tissue
   - Microcalcifications present

3. SENTINEL LYMPH NODES (2 identified):
   - Node 1: Benign with reactive changes (0/1 positive)
   - Node 2: Benign with reactive changes (0/1 positive)

4. AXILLARY LYMPH NODES: 
   - Total examined: 12
   - Positive: 1 (metastatic deposit 3 mm)
   - Other nodes: Show reactive changes only

5. HORMONE AND HER2 STATUS:
   - ER: Positive, 92% of tumor cells
   - PR: Positive, 85% of tumor cells  
   - HER2: Negative (0-1+ score)
   - Ki67: 12% proliferation index (low)

6. TUMOR MARGINS: All margins clear of malignancy (minimum 4 mm)

7. ADIPOSE TISSUE & DERMIS: No tumor involvement identified

PATHOLOGIC STAGE: 
pT1c (2.2 cm), pN1mi (micrometastasis), M0
Stage IIA (T1c, N1)

FINAL DIAGNOSIS SUMMARY:
Low-grade invasive ductal carcinoma with favorable hormone receptor expression, single micrometastatic lymph node. Candidate for hormone therapy without chemotherapy (based on size, grade, and molecular profile).

RECOMMENDATIONS: Adjuvant hormone therapy (tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitor). Molecular subtyping (Oncotype DX or similar) may assist with adjuvant therapy decisions.
